mechanism: Glucose enters glycolysis through Pgi-catalysed isomerisation to F6P, split by aldolase to DHAP and GAP (G3P). G3P is one of the two MEP pathway precursors.
evidence_sentence: glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate (G3P) and pyruvate are condensed to form 1-deoxy-d-xylulose-5-phosphate (DXP) by DXP synthase (DXS)

mechanism: Glucose is catabolised through glycolysis (Embden-Meyerhof-Parnas pathway) to pyruvate, one of the two MEP pathway substrates.
evidence_sentence: deletion of the zwf gene encoding glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase (G6PD)...resulted in the increased carbon fluxes in the Embden-Meyerhof-Parnas (EMP) pathway and indirectly led to an increase in lycopene production

mechanism: Glycerol is phosphorylated to glycerol-3-phosphate then oxidised to DHAP, which enters glycolysis and becomes G3P, elevating the G3P pool relative to pyruvate.
evidence_sentence: Glycerol is a viable carbon source for beta-carotene and lycopene production, as it increases glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate and pyruvate, which are imperative intermediates in central carbon metabolism extension to the MEP pathway

mechanism: Pgi (phosphoglucose isomerase) directs G6P into glycolysis, feeding G3P. KO of pgi blocks glycolytic flux and redirects carbon through pentose phosphate pathway (6PG -> Ru5P -> G3P+E4P), altering the G3P/pyruvate balance.
evidence_sentence: Deletion of pgi and zwf was found to eliminate catabolite repression as well as the ability of E. coli to consume glucose for biomass formation, and the yield from glucose of the bioproduct D-glucaric acid was significantly increased in a delta-pgi delta-zwf strain

mechanism: Zwf (G6P dehydrogenase) diverts G6P into the oxidative pentose phosphate pathway, away from glycolysis. Knockout of zwf redirects more G6P into EMP and raises G3P supply to DXS; zwf deletion has been shown to increase lycopene 130% and beta-carotene 32.5%.
evidence_sentence: knockout of the zwf gene, significantly enhanced lycopene production by over 130%

mechanism: GapA (GAPDH) consumes G3P to 1,3-bisphosphoglycerate in lower glycolysis. Fine reduction of gapA expression raises the G3P pool available to DXS.
evidence_sentence: the distribution between pyruvate and glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate (G3P), the originating precursors of the isoprenoid pathway, is a major factor that can limit isoprenoid production yields in E. coli, with alterations in the central metabolism that redirect flux from pyruvate back to G3P enhance lycopene production

mechanism: PykA (pyruvate kinase II) converts PEP to pyruvate. Deletion of pykA diverts PEP away from pyruvate (and acetate), potentially rebalancing G3P:pyruvate for MEP pathway.
evidence_sentence: deleting pyruvate kinases-I and -II (Pyk-I and-II)...lycopene production was significantly increased

mechanism: PykF (pyruvate kinase I) is the major pyruvate kinase. Deletion of both pykA and pykF nearly eliminates PEP->pyruvate flux through this route, forcing rerouting through PPC/PPS/OAA.
evidence_sentence: deleting pyruvate kinases-I and -II (Pyk-I and-II)...lycopene production was significantly increased

mechanism: Pps (PEP synthase, ppsA) converts pyruvate back to PEP, reducing excess pyruvate and rebalancing flux toward G3P. OE of ppsA produced a 250% increase in lycopene by diverting pyruvate to G3P via PEP.
evidence_sentence: overexpressing phosphoenolpyruvate (PEP) synthase (PPS)...achieved a 250% increase in lycopene production by...diverting flow from pyruvate to G3P

mechanism: By converting pyruvate to PEP, Pps supports gluconeogenic flux that replenishes the G3P pool (PEP -> OAA -> ... or PEP -> F1,6BP -> G3P via reversible glycolytic steps).
evidence_sentence: alterations in the central metabolism that redirect flux from pyruvate back to G3P enhance lycopene production

mechanism: Ppc (PEP carboxylase) converts PEP to oxaloacetate, creating a bypass to the TCA cycle that reduces acetyl-CoA overflow and stabilises the PEP-pyruvate pool.
evidence_sentence: overexpressing Ppc and Pck to introduce the bypass pathway between PEP and pyruvate via the oxaloacetate and TCA cycle

mechanism: AceE is the E1 component of pyruvate dehydrogenase (PDH), converting pyruvate to acetyl-CoA. KO of aceE forces pyruvate accumulation and reroutes flux; paradoxically this can improve lycopene in MEP-dominant systems by restoring pyruvate-to-DXS flux.
evidence_sentence: knockout of aceE would presumably improve carbon flux to pyruvate and formate, and further deletion of fdhF

mechanism: FdhF (formate dehydrogenase H) consumes formate; deletion alters C1 and NADPH pools; combined aceE/fdhF/gdhA triple-KO gave ~40% lycopene improvement.
evidence_sentence: seven mutants...identified...37% improvement in lycopene yield in a gdhA/aceE/fdhF triple-knockout construct

mechanism: GdhA (glutamate dehydrogenase, NADPH-dependent) consumes NADPH to produce glutamate. KO of gdhA spares NADPH for MEP pathway reductions (IspC, IspG, IspH, HMGR) and downstream crtI desaturation.
evidence_sentence: gdhA deletion could increase the availability of NADPH

mechanism: Pta (phosphotransacetylase) converts acetyl-CoA to acetyl-phosphate, leading to acetate excretion. KO reduces acetate overflow but also affects acetyl-CoA availability for MVA pathway.
evidence_sentence: deletion of the acetate and lactate production pathways...elimination of the acetate pathway

mechanism: Ldh (lactate dehydrogenase) consumes pyruvate to lactate under microaerobic conditions. KO reduces pyruvate drain.
evidence_sentence: lactate production pathways

mechanism: The MVA pathway initiates with 3x acetyl-CoA, condensed by AtoB (thiolase) and HMGS (HMG-CoA synthase), then reduced by HMGR to mevalonate.
evidence_sentence: MVA pathway initiates with acetyl-CoA, which is converted into MVA through three reactions, catalyzed by acetoacetyl-CoA thiolase (ACCT), 3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl-CoA (HMG-CoA) synthase (HMGS)

mechanism: AtoB / MvaE (acetoacetyl-CoA thiolase) catalyses the first committed step of MVA (2 acetyl-CoA -> acetoacetyl-CoA). Heterologously expressed from E. faecalis (as mvaE bifunctional) or S. cerevisiae.
evidence_sentence: mvaE and mvaS...catalyzes the conversion from acetyl-CoA to mevalonic acid

mechanism: HMGS / MvaS (HMG-CoA synthase) condenses acetoacetyl-CoA + acetyl-CoA -> HMG-CoA. Heterologously expressed from E. faecalis or S. cerevisiae.
evidence_sentence: catalyzed by acetoacetyl-CoA thiolase (ACCT), 3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl-CoA (HMG-CoA) synthase (HMGS) and HMG-CoA reductase (HMGR)

mechanism: HMGR (HMG-CoA reductase) reduces HMG-CoA to mevalonate using 2x NADPH. Truncated form (tHMGR) lacking membrane anchor is commonly used and more active; heterologously from S. cerevisiae, E. faecalis, or X. dendrorhous.
evidence_sentence: overexpressed tHMGR from X. dendrorhous, a truncated form of HMGR...2.2-fold enhancement in beta-carotene production

mechanism: HMGR requires 2 NADPH per mevalonate. NADPH availability is rate-limiting; MVA pathway consumes 2 NADPH per IPP vs MEP which produces net NADH but consumes NADPH for IspG/IspH.
evidence_sentence: MVA pathway needs 2 NADPH for the production of mevalonate

mechanism: MVK (mevalonate kinase) phosphorylates mevalonate to MVA-5-P; subsequent kinase (PMK/MvaK2) and decarboxylase (MVD/MvaD) yield IPP. MVK was identified as rate-limiting and improved by directed evolution.
evidence_sentence: Mevalonate kinase (MK) is one of the rate-limiting enzymes in the lycopene synthetic pathway

mechanism: PMK (phosphomevalonate kinase, MvaK2) phosphorylates MVA-5-P to MVA-5-PP.
evidence_sentence: two reactions successively catalyzed by MVAP kinase (PMK) and MVAPP decarboxylase (MDD)

mechanism: MVD (mevalonate diphosphate decarboxylase, MvaD) catalyses decarboxylation-dehydration of MVA-5-PP to IPP.
evidence_sentence: MVAPP decarboxylase (MDD)

mechanism: DXS (1-deoxy-D-xylulose-5-phosphate synthase) condenses G3P + pyruvate -> DXP, the first and main rate-limiting step of the MEP pathway. Overexpression gives 3.5x lycopene. Canonical dxs OE studies: Kim & Keasling 2001; Yuan 2006.
evidence_sentence: the step that limits the rate of the MEP pathway...overexpression of dxs in E. coli carotenoid production was enhanced 3.5-fold

mechanism: G3P is a direct substrate of DXS that enters the MEP pathway and becomes IPP via the 8-enzyme cascade.
evidence_sentence: glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate (G3P) and pyruvate are condensed to form 1-deoxy-d-xylulose-5-phosphate (DXP) by DXP synthase (DXS)

mechanism: Pyruvate is the second substrate of DXS, contributing one carbon to DXP and thereby to IPP.
evidence_sentence: glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate (G3P) and pyruvate are condensed to form 1-deoxy-d-xylulose-5-phosphate (DXP) by DXP synthase (DXS)

mechanism: Dxr (= IspC, DXP reductoisomerase) reduces DXP to MEP using NADPH. Rate-limiting co-target with Dxs; combined dxs+dxr OE yielded 22 mg/L lycopene.
evidence_sentence: highest lycopene yield (22 mg/L) was reached with the arabinose-inducible promoter on a medium-copy plasmid pBAD24 in the E. coli XL1-Blue strain

mechanism: IspD (MEP cytidylyltransferase, MCT) converts MEP to CDP-ME. Co-OE with IspF gave 71% astaxanthin increase, indicating MEP rate control at this step too.
evidence_sentence: overabundance of the IspD (MCT) and IspF (MDS) enzymes in the MEP pathway led to a significant boost of 71% in the synthesis of astaxanthin

mechanism: IspE (CDP-ME kinase) phosphorylates CDP-ME to CDP-ME-2P in the MEP pathway.
evidence_sentence: a series of enzymes...successively catalyzes the conversion of MEP into IPP

mechanism: IspF (MECDP synthase, MDS) cyclises CDP-ME-2P to MEcDP. Co-OE with IspD is synergistic.
evidence_sentence: overabundance of the IspD (MCT) and IspF (MDS) enzymes in the MEP pathway led to a significant boost of 71% in the synthesis of astaxanthin

mechanism: IspG (HMB-PP synthase) converts MEcDP to HMB-PP using 2e- + 2H+ (Fe-S cluster; NADPH via flavodoxin).
evidence_sentence: a series of enzymes...successively catalyzes the conversion of MEP into IPP

mechanism: IspH (HMB-PP reductase) converts HMB-PP to IPP+DMAPP directly in an ~5:1 ratio.
evidence_sentence: a series of enzymes...successively catalyzes the conversion of MEP into IPP

mechanism: IspH generates both IPP and DMAPP in ~5:1 ratio.
evidence_sentence: a series of enzymes...successively catalyzes the conversion of MEP into IPP

mechanism: Idi (IPP isomerase) interconverts IPP and DMAPP, equilibrating the pools. Since CrtE requires DMAPP (as well as 3x IPP) to form GGPP, Idi is rate-limiting; OE gives ~1.8x lycopene; co-OE with Dxs is synergistic.
evidence_sentence: IDI is a crucial enzyme...mutant IDI exhibited a 1.8-fold increase in lycopene production

mechanism: IPP is isomerised to DMAPP by Idi (and also generated directly by IspH); DMAPP pool depends on IPP availability.
evidence_sentence: isopentenyl-diphosphate isomerase (IDI) catalyzes the isomerization of IPP to DMAPP

mechanism: IspA (FPP synthase) condenses DMAPP+IPP -> GPP and GPP+IPP -> FPP. Drains the IPP/DMAPP pool toward C15 FPP for essential cellular processes and as CrtE substrate.
evidence_sentence: farnesyl pyrophosphate (FPP) is formed from GPP catalyzed by FPP synthase (IspA)

mechanism: IspA catalyses DMAPP+IPP -> GPP as the first condensation before FPP.
evidence_sentence: condensation reaction between the two intermediates occurs with the formation of geranyl diphosphate (GPP)

mechanism: FPP synthesis requires 2x IPP + DMAPP via IspA-catalysed condensations.
evidence_sentence: FPP is then catalyzed by geranylgeranyl diphosphate synthase (CrtE), phytoene synthase (CrtB), and phytoene desaturase (CrtI) to successively form GGPP, phytoene, and finally lycopene

mechanism: DMAPP is the starter unit for IspA-catalysed condensations forming FPP.
evidence_sentence: isopentenyl pyrophosphate (IPP), dimethylallyl pyrophosphate (DMAPP) and farnesyl pyrophosphate (FPP) are synthesized by 8 sequential enzymes in the MEP pathway, after which they are converted to lycopene

mechanism: IspB (octaprenyl-PP synthase) consumes FPP+IPP to produce octaprenyl-PP (C40 ubiquinone precursor), competing with CrtE for FPP. RBS-downregulation of ispB reduces this competing sink and increases carotenoid titer.
evidence_sentence: FPP, in turn, is catalyzed by geranylgeranyl diphosphate (GGPP) synthase (CrtE)

mechanism: CrtE (GGPP synthase) condenses FPP + IPP -> GGPP. Heterologously expressed from Pantoea ananatis / P. agglomerans / Erwinia uredovora. Rate-limiting step in carotenoid biosynthesis; OE gave 60% lycopene increase.
evidence_sentence: transformation of DMAPP into GGPP...was recognized as the pivotal step that restricts the rate of carotenoid biosynthesis...enhanced the expression of CrtE...60% increase in lycopene production

mechanism: FPP + IPP -> GGPP by CrtE.
evidence_sentence: FPP, in turn, is catalyzed by geranylgeranyl diphosphate (GGPP) synthase (CrtE)

mechanism: CrtB (phytoene synthase) condenses 2x GGPP -> phytoene. Essential heterologous enzyme of the lycopene cassette.
evidence_sentence: phytoene synthase (CrtB)...successively form...phytoene

mechanism: Phytoene formation requires 2x GGPP via CrtB.
evidence_sentence: FPP is then catalyzed by geranylgeranyl diphosphate synthase (CrtE), phytoene synthase (CrtB), and phytoene desaturase (CrtI) to successively form GGPP, phytoene, and finally lycopene

mechanism: CrtI (phytoene desaturase) performs four desaturation steps on phytoene, forming lycopene. FAD-dependent enzyme; directed evolution (Y160F+N576S mutant) improved lycopene 60%.
evidence_sentence: phytoene desaturase (CrtI) to successively form...finally lycopene

mechanism: Phytoene is the direct substrate of CrtI to form lycopene via four desaturations.
evidence_sentence: phytoene desaturase (CrtI) to successively form...finally lycopene

mechanism: Shortcut edge: higher GGPP pool results in higher lycopene flux. Included to allow BUILDER to use a compact topology if metabolite pools are collapsed.
evidence_sentence: transformation of DMAPP into GGPP...was recognized as the pivotal step that restricts the rate of carotenoid biosynthesis

mechanism: Shortcut edge: FPP pool ultimately feeds lycopene. Included as optional BUILDER-selectable compact edge.
evidence_sentence: FPP is then catalyzed by geranylgeranyl diphosphate synthase (CrtE), phytoene synthase (CrtB), and phytoene desaturase (CrtI) to successively form GGPP, phytoene, and finally lycopene

mechanism: IPTG induces lac/trc/T7 promoters driving heterologous dxs expression when under lac-controlled expression system.
evidence_sentence: highest lycopene yield (22 mg/L) was reached with the arabinose-inducible promoter on a medium-copy plasmid pBAD24 in the E. coli XL1-Blue strain

mechanism: IPTG induces lac/trc/T7 promoters controlling idi overexpression in standard plasmid systems.
evidence_sentence: RBS libraries...leading to an increase in lycopene yield by 32%

mechanism: IPTG induces the lac/T7 promoter driving heterologous crt cassette expression in standard vectors.
evidence_sentence: E. coli harboring the P. agglomerans crt genes produced 27 mg/l of lycopene in 2YT medium without IPTG induction, which was twofold higher than that produced by E. coli harboring the P. ananatis crt genes (12 mg/l lycopene) with 0.1 mM IPTG induction

mechanism: Same promoter system as CrtE; IPTG induces lac/T7-driven crtB expression.
evidence_sentence: E. coli harboring the P. agglomerans crt genes produced 27 mg/l of lycopene in 2YT medium without IPTG induction

mechanism: Same promoter system; IPTG induces crtI expression in lac/T7 cassettes.
evidence_sentence: 0.1 mM IPTG induction

mechanism: PBAD promoter (AraC regulator) induces dxs when cloned on pBAD24/pA-SRAI vectors; 1% L-arabinose gives strong induction.
evidence_sentence: dxs, dxr, ispA, and idi from E. coli...expressed under the PBAD promoter regulated by AraC protein and arabinose

mechanism: Same PBAD system; arabinose induces idi coexpressed in pBAD cassettes.
evidence_sentence: dxs, dxr, ispA, and idi from E. coli...expressed under the PBAD promoter regulated by AraC protein and arabinose

mechanism: Arabinose induces PBAD-driven dxr OE cassettes.
evidence_sentence: dxs, dxr, ispA, and idi from E. coli...expressed under the PBAD promoter regulated by AraC protein and arabinose

mechanism: Arabinose induces PBAD-driven ispA OE plasmids.
evidence_sentence: dxs, dxr, ispA, and idi from E. coli...expressed under the PBAD promoter regulated by AraC protein and arabinose

mechanism: Higher plasmid copy number (e.g., pACYC p15A ~15 copies, pBR322 ~20 copies, pBluescript ~500 copies) increases crtE gene dosage and protein abundance.
evidence_sentence: one copy of MVA upper pathway, two copies of MVA lower pathway, and three copies of lycopene expression pathway was comprehended as the best performer

mechanism: Same mechanism; copy number tunes CrtB abundance.
evidence_sentence: three copies of lycopene expression pathway was comprehended as the best performer

mechanism: Same mechanism; copy number tunes CrtI abundance.
evidence_sentence: three copies of lycopene expression pathway was comprehended as the best performer

mechanism: CRP (cAMP receptor protein) is a global TF modulating hundreds of genes via cAMP-CRP complex. Engineered CRP variants (site-directed mutagenesis) gave ~25% more lycopene by fine-tuning central metabolism for precursor supply.
evidence_sentence: global regulator cAMP receptor protein (CRP)...transcriptional engineering...ultimately leading to improved lycopene production...approximately 25% more lycopene

mechanism: RpoS (sigma-S) is the stationary-phase sigma factor regulating ~10% of E. coli genome including katE catalase. Î”rpos strains accumulate ROS that chemically degrade lycopene, giving ~8-fold lower titer in stationary phase. Does not directly regulate MEP enzymes.
evidence_sentence: knockout of rpoS does not influence flux through the MEP pathway...increased accumulation of reactive oxygen species during late log and stationary phases

mechanism: RpoS directly transcribes katE (stationary-phase catalase), which degrades H2O2 and limits ROS damage to lycopene.
evidence_sentence: concentration of the stationary phase-specific catalase KatE...was reduced >twofold in MG1655*

mechanism: KatE (catalase HP-II) degrades hydrogen peroxide, lowering ROS level.
evidence_sentence: overexpression of katE increased measurable lycopene in MG1655*, reducing the difference...to less than two-fold from previously eightfold

mechanism: ROS (H2O2, superoxide, hydroxyl radical) chemically oxidise lycopene's conjugated double bonds, degrading it and lowering measurable titer.
evidence_sentence: measurable lycopene content decreased significantly in the presence of sublethal doses of menadione

mechanism: SoxS (activated by SoxR upon superoxide/redox-cycler exposure) transcriptionally activates zwf, fpr, fumC. This means oxidative stress raises Zwf, redirecting carbon into PPP and away from MEP precursors â€” secondary anti-lycopene route.
evidence_sentence: Expression of zwf gene that encodes the first key enzyme from the pentose phosphate pathway is among others controlled by the SoxRS regulon in response to oxidative stress

mechanism: SoxR is a 2Fe-2S-cluster sensor of redox-cycling compounds (paraquat, menadione); oxidised SoxR activates soxS transcription.
evidence_sentence: The SoxRS regulon is a response mechanism to O2- and to redox-cycling compounds, but not to H2O2

mechanism: OxyR senses H2O2 via disulfide bond formation; activates katG, ahpCF, gorA, dps genes for H2O2 defense.
evidence_sentence: The OxyR system (which responds to H2O2)

mechanism: AppY (AraC-family TF for anaerobic energy metabolism genes) enhances lycopene when co-expressed with dxs â€” 8-fold improvement in one study.
evidence_sentence: co-expression of appY with dxs produced an eight-fold improvement in lycopene yield

mechanism: Crl (sigma-S chaperone) increases RpoS association with core RNAP, stabilising stationary-phase transcription program and indirectly boosting lycopene via RpoS->KatE->ROS defense.
evidence_sentence: Crl (transcriptional regulator of csgBA for curli surface fiber formation) also enhanced lycopene production

mechanism: Hnr (RssB) adapter protein targets RpoS for ClpXP degradation. Î”hnr stabilises RpoS, increasing lycopene; double KO with Î”yliE gives significant improvement.
evidence_sentence: Î”hnr Î”yliE mutant...significantly improved lycopene production

mechanism: YliE is a c-di-GMP phosphodiesterase; Î”yliE enhances lycopene in a Î”hnr background. Mechanism likely via biofilm/stress phenotypes modulating RpoS stability.
evidence_sentence: Î”hnr Î”yliE mutant...significantly improved lycopene production

mechanism: ArcA (anoxic redox control, two-component response regulator with ArcB) represses TCA and PDH genes including aceE under microaerobic conditions.
evidence_sentence: knockout of aceE would presumably improve carbon flux to pyruvate and formate

mechanism: FNR (4Fe-4S-cluster anaerobic regulator) represses aerobic metabolism genes (including aceE) in anaerobic conditions.
evidence_sentence: knockout of aceE would presumably improve carbon flux to pyruvate

mechanism: ArcB kinase is autophosphorylated under reduced (low O2) conditions; high O2 deactivates ArcA; low O2 activates ArcA (we encode oxygen -> ArcA with sign -1, meaning higher O2 reduces ArcA activity).
evidence_sentence: presumably improve carbon flux to pyruvate and formate

mechanism: FNR Fe-S cluster is O2-labile; FNR is active only under anaerobic conditions. Higher O2 -> lower FNR activity.
evidence_sentence: anaerobic energy metabolism

mechanism: High dissolved O2 increases ROS generation by leaky electron transport chain; excessive aeration causes lycopene photoxidation/chemical oxidation.
evidence_sentence: proteins involved in aerobic respiration and the TCA cycle were increased when compared to Seq+

mechanism: Elevated temperature (37 degC) causes soluble protein misfolding, T7 polymerase-driven metabolic burden, and faster lycopene degradation. Lower culture T (28-30 degC) yields higher lycopene.
evidence_sentence: temperature-shift culture method (37 to 25 degC)...further augment lycopene production

mechanism: Rich media (e.g., 2xYTg with 20% glycerol, tryptone, yeast extract) support higher biomass and lycopene accumulation than minimal media; MG1655 titer 141 mg/L in LB scales to 1595 mg/L in 2xYTg.
evidence_sentence: cells grown in 2xYTg achieved 4.6, 4.1, and 4-fold higher lycopene titers (651 mg/l)...2xYTg titer was 1,595 mg/l

mechanism: K-12 background (MG1655, W3110) has functional RpoS and complete TCA; outperforms B strains (BL21) and DH5a for lycopene 10-16x. Strain lineage is a source node affecting baseline lycopene capacity.
evidence_sentence: MG1655 expressed lycopene production capacity 10 to 16 times higher than commonly used laboratory strains such as DH5a and BL21 (DE3)

mechanism: Plsb (glycerol-3-phosphate acyltransferase) increases membrane lipid biosynthesis, creating hydrophobic compartments for lycopene storage. OE with Plsc and Almgs boosts lycopene to 36.4 mg/g DCW.
evidence_sentence: Plsb (glycerol-3-phosphate acyltransferase) and Plsc...were overexpressed...cells produced sufficient intracellular membrane vesicles

mechanism: Plsc (1-acylglycerol-3-phosphate acyltransferase) cooperates with Plsb in membrane lipid biosynthesis.
evidence_sentence: 1-acylglycerol-3-phosphate-acyltransferase), were overexpressed...cells produced sufficient intracellular membrane vesicles

mechanism: Almgs (monoglucosyl diacylglycerol synthase from Acholeplasma) increases glycerophospholipids, giving 36.4 mg/g DCW lycopene in combination with plsb/plsc.
evidence_sentence: amount of the membrane component, glycerophospholipids, was increased...36.4 mg/g DCW of lycopene accumulated

mechanism: WaaC is an LPS heptosyltransferase. Deletion truncates LPS (deep-rough phenotype), altering outer membrane permeability and promoting lycopene production.
evidence_sentence: deletion of the waaC and waaF genes greatly promoted lycopene production

mechanism: WaaF catalyses second LPS core heptose addition; Î”waaF truncates LPS core, similar to Î”waaC effect.
evidence_sentence: deletion of the waaC and waaF genes greatly promoted lycopene production

mechanism: Lpp (Braun's lipoprotein) tethers peptidoglycan to outer membrane; deletion plus nlpI, mlaE, tolA gave 59.34-fold extracellular lycopene increase.
evidence_sentence: deletion of the lpp, nlpI, mlaE, and tolA genes...59.34-fold improvement in extracellular lycopene production

mechanism: NlpI (lipoprotein-mediated cell-division regulator) deletion destabilises OM; contributes to extracellular lycopene.
evidence_sentence: deletion of the lpp, nlpI, mlaE, and tolA genes...59.34-fold improvement in extracellular lycopene production

mechanism: MlaE (phospholipid retrograde transport component) deletion disrupts OM lipid asymmetry and promotes product efflux.
evidence_sentence: deletion of the lpp, nlpI, mlaE, and tolA genes...59.34-fold improvement in extracellular lycopene production

mechanism: TolA (part of Tol-Pal system for OM-IM contact) deletion destabilises outer membrane.
evidence_sentence: deletion of the lpp, nlpI, mlaE, and tolA genes...59.34-fold improvement in extracellular lycopene production

mechanism: EutD (phosphotransacetylase-like in ethanolamine utilization) consumes acetyl-CoA; deletion preserves acetyl-CoA pool.
evidence_sentence: Deletion of gdhA, eutD; overexpression of tpiA, ompE, and ompN...174% increase in lycopene titer

mechanism: TpiA (triose phosphate isomerase) interconverts DHAP and G3P; OE may increase G3P pool depending on flux direction.
evidence_sentence: Deletion of gdhA, eutD; overexpression of tpiA, ompE, and ompN...174% increase in lycopene titer

mechanism: Shortcut direct edge: Idi OE broadly increases lycopene via IPP-DMAPP pool equilibration feeding downstream cascade. Included for BUILDER option.

mechanism: Shortcut direct edge: Dxs OE is the most studied single-gene intervention, giving 3.5x lycopene.

mechanism: Pgi is required for glycolytic flux from G6P to F6P, eventually producing pyruvate. Î”pgi reroutes carbon through PPP.

evidence_sentence: The ED pathway is a shortcut for G3P and pyruvate generation. It produces the two MEP precursors in equal amounts via a four-step reaction from G6P.

mechanism: Edd contribution to pyruvate pool via the ED pathway shortcut.
evidence_sentence: The ED pathway is a shortcut for G3P and pyruvate generation. It produces the two MEP precursors in equal amounts via a four-step reaction from G6P.

mechanism: Eda (KDPG aldolase) cleaves KDPG into G3P and pyruvate, completing the ED shortcut that feeds MEP precursors 1:1.

mechanism: Gnd (6-phosphogluconate dehydrogenase) supplies NADPH via PPP oxidative branch; NADPH is consumed by IspC/IspG/IspH/CrtI downstream.

mechanism: KatE (HPII catalase) scavenges H2O2; OE protects lycopene from oxidative degradation and elevates measurable titer.
evidence_sentence: katE was decreased twofold in MG1655* and overexpression of katE increased measurable lycopene in MG1655*

mechanism: SodA (Mn-SOD) converts O2- to H2O2; upregulation lowers superoxide-mediated lycopene loss.
evidence_sentence: a 1.5-fold increase was observed for the manganese-dependent superoxide dismutase, SodA

mechanism: Lycopene is an efficient singlet-oxygen quencher (kq ~31e9 M-1 s-1); accumulated lycopene quenches ROS and is partially consumed.
evidence_sentence: Lycopene is a potent antioxidant... The chromogenic conjugated double bonds of lycopene are readily oxidized, leading to loss of the characteristic red color

mechanism: ROS (H2O2, O2-, 1O2) oxidatively degrade lycopene; high intracellular ROS lowers measurable titer independently of biosynthesis flux. Bongers 2015 reinterprets Alper 2005's RpoS effect as largely ROS-mediated pigment loss, not MEP-flux change.
evidence_sentence: Lycopene is a potent antioxidant... The chromogenic conjugated double bonds of lycopene are readily oxidized, leading to loss of the characteristic red color

mechanism: DgkA expands the glycerophospholipid pool; with Plsb/Plsc, enlarges the hydrophobic sink that sequesters lycopene.
evidence_sentence: Plsb (glycerol-3-phosphate-acyltransferase), Plsc (1-acylglycerol-3-phosphate-acyltransferase), and Dgka (diacylglycerol kinase) are the key enzymes of the diglyceride-3-phosphate synthesis pathway

mechanism: Almgs cooperates with Plsb/Plsc OE; combined strain reaches 1.32x specific lycopene and 73.2% membrane localisation (vs 55.4% parent).

mechanism: GPP feedback-inhibits MVK, a downstream-product throttle on upstream MVA flux.

mechanism: FPP feedback-inhibits MVK, limiting MVA throughput when sesquiterpene precursors accumulate.
evidence_sentence: MKs are subject to feedback inhibition by lycopene biosynthesis intermediates, such as geranyldiphosphate (GPP), farnesyldiphosphate (FPP), and geranylgeranyldiphosphate (GGPP)

mechanism: GGPP feedback-inhibits MVK; with GPP/FPP triggers throughput limits when carotenoid precursors build up.

mechanism: Excess GPP is cytotoxic to E. coli; in MVA-overexpressing strains this reduces biomass and total titer.
evidence_sentence: Excessive midstream module flux harmed lycopene accumulation and cell growth, which could be due to the downstream module's limited capacity or the potential cytotoxicity of accumulated intermediate metabolites like GPP and FPP

mechanism: FPP accumulation causes host growth penalty; balanced expression (lower copy, weaker RBS) relieves toxicity and raises titer.

mechanism: Plasmid copy (p15A vs pSC101) sets MVK abundance; lower copy lowers intermediate toxicity.
evidence_sentence: As the replication origin of the midstream module changed from p15A to pSC101 with reducing the copy number, lycopene production further improved

mechanism: Plasmid copy controls Idi abundance; stronger IDI expression consistently improves lycopene (Cheng 2022).
evidence_sentence: IDI activity appeared to be critical for a high lycopene pathway... all the strains showed stronger TIR of RBS-IDI

mechanism: Dxs consumes G3P + pyruvate into DXP; strong Dxs OE depletes G3P. Wang 2009 MAGE finds balanced (not maximal) RBS tuning for dxs/idi/ispD/ispE/ispF gives the >5x optimum, implying an optimum not monotonic activation.
evidence_sentence: applied MAGE to optimize the 1-deoxy-D-xylulose-5-phosphate biosynthesis pathway in E. coli to overproduce lycopene... isolated variants with more than fivefold increase in lycopene production within 3 days

mechanism: Dxs is a substrate consumer of pyruvate; at high expression it competes with pyruvate kinase/Ppc-directed flux.
evidence_sentence: applied MAGE to optimize the 1-deoxy-D-xylulose-5-phosphate biosynthesis pathway in E. coli to overproduce lycopene... isolated variants with more than fivefold increase in lycopene production within 3 days

mechanism: AtoB (MvaE upper) condenses 2 acetyl-CoA to acetoacetyl-CoA, the committed MVA step; ortholog choice (E. saccharolyticus > E. faecalis) adds ~2x titer when paired with S. cerevisiae lower MVA.
evidence_sentence: pSCS3-pLYC produced 77 mg/l lycopene, 8.6-fold higher than pSTV28-pLYC control and 97% higher than pSNA-pLYC

mechanism: PpsA (PEP synthase) recycles pyruvate back to PEP, shifting the G3P:pyruvate ratio toward G3P (via lower glycolysis loop); Farmer & Liao 2000 identify this as a dominant flux control for MEP.
evidence_sentence: the distribution between pyruvate and glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate (G3P), the originating precursors of the isoprenoid pathway, is a major factor that can limit isoprenoid production yields in E. coli, with alterations in the central metabolism that redirect flux from pyruvate back to G3P enhance lycopene production

mechanism: Xylose is catabolised via xylose isomerase -> Xu5P -> PPP -> G3P + pyruvate; as a non-PTS sugar it can bypass glucose-mediated catabolite repression.
evidence_sentence: Xylose is another viable carbon source that feeds glycolysis via xylose isomerase and xylulokinase, supplying pyruvate and G3P to the MEP pathway

mechanism: Xylose supplies G3P through the PPP/glycolysis convergence; relevant for T057 which tests xylose carbon source.
evidence_sentence: Xylose is another viable carbon source that feeds glycolysis via xylose isomerase and xylulokinase, supplying pyruvate and G3P to the MEP pathway
